textify(pte||(pte=(0,Ce.Z)([" “Yeah. She escaped the chromed leyaks, the tainted flowers, and the mercenaries when stronger, tougher soldiers were easily defeated. She must have an ace or two up her sleeves... but it doesn’t delete what she’s done for us. Would you really abandon a victim of the Peers’ folly to her fate? What if she were Lappis, or Harvvey? Wouldn’t you do anything to save her?” The former XO gulps. Doubt clouds her electric blue eyes. “I... you’re right. What she was or what she did in the past doesn’t matter anymore. She was turned into a brainless doll like many others, and that’s unacceptable. Stars, I’m glad Mother’s not here anymore to see just how low I’ve fallen.” You smile. “It’s only when you hit rock bottom that you can pounce back up. We entered this place together, we’ll leave it together. Don’t worry about Zero Plus, I’ll keep an eye on her.” The exceedingly cheerful doctor waves at you from the other side of the room. “Whatcha doin’? We’re almost there!” she shouts. Paulette raises her clawed hand. “We’re coming right up! I was just... a little shaken by the Investigative Viridiplantae Yard.” “Oh gosh, you’re so right! The big booby plant lady was so gorgeous! No wonder you feel sad now. Do you want a hug?” Zero Plus asks, a bright smile on her snowy cheeks. “Maybe later,” Paulette replies. “Will you be okay?” you ask. The chromed zaika nods. “Yes. Here, take these. I found them when we searched for a weedkiller,” she whispers, handing you power potions and emp grenades. “Just in case I lose it again, or try to hurt you. I wouldn’t be surprised if Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] installed an override or kill-switch in her creations — me included.” Is she sure about this? These explosives can damage her limbs and augmented organs alike! “It doesn’t matter. I trust your judgement, [pc.name].” "])))

textify(yte||(yte=(0,Ce.Z)([" You set your eyes on hers. “Have you lost your mind?!” Paulette startles. She’s shaking with fear, on the verge to tears. Stars, she didn’t lie about being on edge. textify(kte||(kte=(0,Ce.Z)([" Day *043: “Finally, I can finally do something with this place! Paragon’s Tech-thralls were easy to hijack. textify(Fte||(Fte=(0,Ce.Z)([" Dabbebieth gasps in shock. “[pc.name], no! Don’t do this to me! Don’t do this to Paulette!” You reach for your weapon. The cobalt jellyfish dodges you at the last second, desperately flailing his tentacles to get enough momentum to avoid your attacks. His big red eyes are soaked with tears, both from Paulette’s death and from your ultimate betrayal. Suddenly, the blue alien tenses. His entire body goes limp as Ovvi’s sharp, chromed tail whips forward and loads him up with tranquilizers. She slowly takes him by the sails, caressing his gelatinous face before sealing him into a bubbling tank. “Most e[silly |gg |x ]cellent. I knew I could count on you, [pc.name].” What now? Ovvi smiles, reaching for her control remote. A round, hovering vehicle floats down from the ceiling. It hovers above the ground a couple of seconds before parking right next to you. “[pc.mf |[silly |Here’s your carriage, M’lord. |And here’s my part of the deal ] |[silly |Madam is served. |Now here’s my part of the deal. ] ] Don’t be shy, hop in! This is my personal hoverorb — certified explosive free! My partners deserve only the best.” “Will you keep your word?” Ovvi smirks, raising the portable tank, with a peacefully slumbering Dabbebieth inside, to her face. She lovingly caresses it, fantasizing about the things she’ll do to her new plaything. “I am many things, [pc.name], but I’m not a betrayer. Those who favor me get favors in return. I’ll offer you Dhaal — no! I’ll deliver the Stars to you on a silver platter, my dear associate! Now go and enjoy my gracious gifts!” Not reassured in the slightest, you step into the chromed machine. As soon as you claim the comfortable seat, the engines rev with power. Ovvi waves at you as a reinforced windshield slides into position. “Farewell [pc.name]! The next time we meet, the galaxy will be ours!” The capsule soars through the sky at supersonic speeds, pinning you to the seat as it flies over the Crucible towards the planet’s equator. Clouds explode as you pass through them, bursting into millions of tiny droplets that rain down to the arid soil below. In the blink of an eye, the zaika megalopolis appears in front of you. The towering skyscrapers and flashy buildings of the Meridian contrast strongly with the crumbling ruins and fuming factories of the Farrow. Your vessel casually floats over the lowlands in the direction of the spaceport. You don’t know how, but the red and silver vehicle knows exactly which ship to look for. Maybe Ovvi secretly hacked your Codex during the flight? After a minute of slow hovering, the machine lands a few feet away from the [pc_ship.short]. The windshield slides down, releasing you from Ovvi’s grasp. You carefully step out of it, panting deeply. The trip left you weakened and breathless. You’re lucky the crushing speed didn’t break any of your bones. The machine lifts up a little, hovering above the ground. A hidden compartment opens under it, revealing a polished crate bearing Dr. [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s gifts. Antigrav generators slowly place the first of Ovvi’s rewards in front of you. The capsule beeps and boops a couple more times, judging you wordlessly before speeding off into the horizon. You slowly pry the crate open. You gawk in shock at the sight of all the riches inside: platinum, gemstones, power armor, experimental weapons — all you need to kickstart a small mercenary company! Looking carefully, you notice a mini datapad under all the gold and jewels. Without further ado, you reach for the sleek device and turn it on. A single message appears on screen. “Hope you like my first gift, [pc.name]. As long as you breathe, you and your offspring will be [pc.mf |kings and queens among mortals — Gods even! |queens and kings among mortals — Goddesses even! ] Don’t worry about your friend. His glorious form will be nothing like these pathetic T4L0Ns. He’ll be... perfect!” -Yours, Ovvi. There’s a small P.S.: “You are, of course, invited to the grand opening of my future planet-wide pleasure resort: OvviLand!” Truly despicable of her. You made a pact with the devil and will now have to live with it. Let’s hope she won’t be a pain in your side before you get your inheritance back. You never know when she may double cross you — someone learned that the hard way today. At least you’re safe and sound, and that’s all that matters. Some will call you a coward, others an opportunist, but those are far from the truth. You are whole, complete... unlike Ovvi’s broken creations. "])))

Our legal and communication departments will be in for a rough night. Bring the ship to low orbit, our recovery teams will pick you up and secure it from here.” You reach for your Codex and send him pictures and files of the many things you encountered onboard. “We came across many mechanised leyaks and modded lifeforms — proceed with extreme caution. There should be two lifeforms near one of the hull accesses, another in the Psionic Enhancement Ward, two huge ones in the Investigative Viridiplantae Yard and Main Lab respectively, along with an unknown amount of roaming leyaks, and the four of us in the Restricted Lab.” “The four of you?” Paulette and Dabbebieth wave at Darius. “The more the merrier,” the former XO says. “And who’s the fourth one?” You frown. Before you can explain, Paulette reaches for the speaker. “Zero Plus, another victim of Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s experiments. She helped us take control of her Mistress’s scientific vessel.” [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |The zaika in question bounces in front of the screen. “Heyo Mr. Robot man! How are you?” “Fine,” Darius casually answers. “Would you kindly move aside Ms. Plus? [pc.name] and I still have some things to settle.” Zero Plus giggles. “See you later, cutie!” |Behind you, Ovvi shudders. “T-Thanks Paulette. I don’t deserve it.” The former XO leaves the range of the screen and kneels in front of the tied Peer. “It’s because you don’t deserve it that you must earn it. No place in this galaxy will be safe, if you misbehave.” The defeated doctor wordlessly nods. Let’s hope she doesn’t start another mad project once your back is turned. ] “Our teams will extensively study and disarm this zaika galleon before its induction into the Steele Tech fleet. The scientific teams begged the financial department for a brand new science vessel — Dr. Lessau will be thrilled to have an early Xmas present. The recovery teams are inbound. They should arrive in two hours. If I were you, I’d find something to do in the meantime.” “See you on Dhaal, Darius.” “Safe travels [pc.name],” he replies before hanging down. You slowly turn the chair around to face Paulette and Dabbebieth. “We’ve got some time to unwind.” Paulette cocks an eyebrow. “Oh really? Well, I know exactly how to pass the time.” "])))

textify(Qte||(Qte=(0,Ce.Z)([" [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus gasps. “Watcha gonna do, Paullie Pally? We’re gonna play ga[silly |y ]mes?” “Not quite,” she mysteriously answers. |“What’s on your mind?” “Something neat,” she mysteriously answers. ] She leans over the desk and reaches for the keyboard. Her orange hand dances on the device in a blur of light. She scours the secret files, riding streams of raw, encrypted data as casually as she breathes unsteadily. After a minute of intense searching, you hear a hiss of pressurized air. A reinforced safe opens on the other side of the room. “Is it?” “Yes. The cabinet containing the GUs’ mnemonic locks. The key to my lost memories is here, a few steps away. I can almost feel it.” Dabbebieth floats between you. “Which one was it again? Case SR-369?” Paulette nods. “Indeed. I’ll finally know the truth about myself.” Without further ado, you step toward the huge safe. There’s hundreds of drawers. Each and everyone of them is filled by one of Ovvi’s invention: a Heisenberg-Doppler shield emitter here, a foldable energy scythe there, schematics for new engines, new power sources, new genetic therapies, new species — she studied and covered all the fields related to terraformation and space exploration. She didn’t lie about bringing zaika civilization to the next level! “MI- OP- Almost there,” Dabbebieth mutters, searching dozens of drawers at once with his lith tentacles. Next to you, Paulette slowly stares at the safe. You don’t know if she’s flabbergasted by Ovvi’s work or scared by the sheer amount of stolen data locked here, in Dr. [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s sanctum. In the blink of an eye, the former XO reaches for a small compartment near the bottom. The steel drawer seamlessly slides open, revealing a scanner-like device. A little tag identifies it as Mnemonic lockpick SR-369. GUs 1 to 17. Handle with care. “Finally. The key to my mind. We went through Hell and back to find it.” Indeed. Does she know how to use it? Behind you, [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus pounces on her feet. “Me! Me! Me! I know how it works!” Paulette cocks an eyebrow. “Show me.” The bimbo nurse bounces next to her and takes the device from her hand. “You hold it against your head, scan your temple, then it zaps your brain where it’s all tangly!” |Ovvi grunts. “Hold it against your temple. It’ll untie the neuronal knot in your superego with a small jolt.” ] Your crewmate shrugs. “Ominous as expected. It doesn’t look strong enough to fry my brain or pierce my reinforced skull. It’s worth a try.” “Maybe sit down first,” Dabbebieth proposes. “You might be overwhelmed by the emotional feedback.” “That’s not a bad idea. You’ve become a lot more protective as of late,” she remarks, petting her friend’s helmeted head. The blue jellyfish blushes brightly. It’s heartwarming to see how far he’s grown from a scared leyak into a cosmic horror slayer. Without further ado, the former XO takes your place at the desk and plugs herself to the computer. Above, a projector sparks to life, beaming images from her systems on a bright holoscreen. “You deserve to see it, after all we went through,” Paulette comments before raising the mnemonic key to her temple. “It better not sting too much. I hate sudden jolts.” The device extends slowly, placing a little electrode on your crewmate’s scalp before deploying little antennas. They start flickering a moment later, filling the room with more orange lights. Paulette’s eyes go wide. They move left and right, up and down, unable to focus on anyone or anything. On the screen, dozens of images appear in quick succession. Most of them are mundane, featuring nothing but Lithium Flower rambling like an angsty teen to her faithful bodyguard. Stars, this girl had issues. Next to you, [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus [silly |shares some popcorn with Dabbebieth, observing |and Dabbebieth attentively observe ] your friend’s revealed memories |Dabbebieth attentively observes your friend’s memories[silly | with a bag of popcorn. Wait, where did he get it? |. ] ] Suddenly, the display settles on a familiar scene. You recognize the setting: Ovvi’s Main Lab. The cruel Peer is nowhere to be seen, but you can clearly see Lithium Flower meddling with Paulette’s gear. “Alright GU-1, let’s see how you fare with this one.” A threat appears on past-Paulette’s visor. She swiftly turns around and raises her rocket-propelled fist at it. “[silly |Stop right there, you criminal scum! |Target acquired. Neutralizing! ]” she blares in a voice nothing like hers before lunging at the training robot. The machine suddenly reveals hardlight claws, buzzing saws, and laser weaponry that’d put the U.G.C’s special forces to shame. She nimbly evades the deadly beams and serrated blades, using her thrusters to perform daring dodges and mad swirls. In the blink of an eye, she punches the poor android’s arms off, leaving only its chest, legs, and head intact. “Nicely done GU-1. Now, terminate it.” “Compliance,” she answers, raising her fist at its face. She steps closer, revving her jet booster for a killing blow. Her visor flickers. Something’s not right. “C-Comply...ance.” Her arm stops its course at the last moment, missing the machine by a hair’s breadth. “Com- Comply- yance.” Lithium Flower stalks toward GU-1 on chromed tentacles. “Not again! The damn inhibitor’s on overdrive! You should be an obedient dolly! This droid killed your loved ones, all of them! It deserves no compassion, no mercy! What’s wrong with you?” Past Paulette doesn’t respond. You read no error on her display. Her body’s drugged and altered beyond acceptable limits and yet a spark of independent life remains in her. “I tried everything. Electroshocks, chemical overload, invasive surgery, but nothing seems to erase your subconscious entirely. You are a mystery GU-1. How did you survive [silly |the XO program cranked to 11? |what should have broken a regular XO tenfold? ]” Just then, Lithium Flower’s holophone rings. “Yes boss, whatsup?” “Not us, not yet at least. Did it work this time?” The bratty zaika frowns. “No. She spared it again. No matter the calibrations, she refuses to kill. For some reason, her body — as mutilated and altered as is — refuses to terminate another being, even an artificial one. I wonder why.” You hear Ovvi hum through the speaker. “Maybe it’s because of her mental state when she was indoctrinated. She wasn’t very stable when we got her.” Lithium Flower nods. “I remember perfectly how desperate she was. She needed a huge amount of money to protect her mother’s legacy. She was in tears through the entire interview. If she suffered loss just before her transformation, maybe it inadvertently conflicted with our treatment and subconsciously added a protection imperative to her body?” “If that’s the case, then there’s nothing we can do to fix her. GU-1 is a failure. Scrap her.” The small zaika gasps. “I-I can do it! We can remake her again — we have the technology.” Ovvi hisses. “You can keep her if you want. A merciful GU is no use to me. We’ll start GU-2’s indoctrination tomorrow, starting with the brainwashing this time. Don’t forget to remove this conversation from her logs before processing her. Ovvi over.” Lithium Flower sighs. “I’m sorry GU-1. You won’t be the [silly |Ultimate Lifeform |next step to zaika ev[silly |i |o ]lution ],” she says, sincerely disappointed. “Initiate protocol SR-369.” The projector turns down. Next to you, Paulette shakes her head. “I-It’s so clear to me now. I didn’t remember that she was the one that hooked me to their project. Stars, I hope Lappis got the money in the end.” Dabbebieth floats down to Paulette. “Since the Little Blossoms Shelter is still running, she certainly did. How are you feeling?” The amazonian cyborg shrugs. “I don’t know. On the one hand, I’m creeped out by all the stuff they hid from me, but on the other hand, I’m glad that they couldn’t erase me, no matter how hard they tried.” “Maybe your mother’s memories kept you alive, in spite of all the mutilations you went through.” “Yeah... Most of my logs are confessions and diaries from Lithium Flower. I... don’t feel like sharing this stuff. It feels like violating her privacy. Wait... the last two passages triggered after Dabbebieth’s capture and aboard the Gloaming!” The shy leyak raises a tentacle. “Here they are, the memories we were looking for! We’ll finally know what happened, back then.” Paulette breathes deeply. “Alright. Buckle up everyone, this one’s gonna be a rough ride.” "])))

textify(Jte||(Jte=(0,Ce.Z)([" The screen turns back online, this time in an unknown but extremely fancy and futuristic place. “Lithium Flower’s meridian apartments,” Paulette murmurs. You hear a clamor coming from behind and metal clicking, as if you were being chased by a mechanical spider. Past Paulette’s visor recording turns to an infuriated Lithium Flower. The purple brat is fuming in front of an exquisite tank filled with rare sea creatures. You recognize some galactic species but a good number of them are completely unknown to your Codex. How many secrets did the Peer hide from the U.G.C? “I’ll make them pay for this! No one steals from me without facing the consequences!” she barks before turning to face Past Paulette. “Finally! GU-1, I command you to track Tjulmo and punish its captors — thoroughly. Use all your weapons to recover it.” “Roger,” the GU simply replies. She mechanically turns around and activates her sensors. Interestingly, the thieves left an ultraviolet signature on everything they touched. Someone’s got defective, leaking bionics! “Wait!” the shortstack zaika shouts from across the room. Past Paulette stops in her tracks. “Yes Mistress?” “Protocol RS-963. If your systems deem the rescue too dangerous, reject my initial orders and go back home. I don’t want to lose you too. Go — my flame — and give them hell!” The screen goes black. Well, that’s a surprise: Lithium Flower has a heart, albeit a very wicked one. She... cared for Paulette, or rather, the obedient, modded version of her. “I didn’t expect that,” your crewmate admits. “After all she’s done to me, all the pain, the humiliation, and forced alterations, I feel... no, I don’t feel. I should be angry, sad, vengeful, but I’m none of that — I’m just... mildly annoyed.” Dabbebieth taps her shoulder. “We beat her fair and square. Lithium Flower has no power over us anymore. The nightmare’s over.” What does Tjulmo mean? You heard Lithium Flower call Dabbebieth that name. “It’s old zaika for treasure, precious, extraordinary. She considered him very valuable,” Paulette replies, “but not enough to care about his health and rights as a living being.” The shy leyak sighs. “Like most leyak owners. Anyway, let’s check the last file. I wanna know how this story ends.” "])))

original: textify(toe||(toe=(0,Ce.Z)([" “Dhaal’s landing site is in view! We’ll arrive in about two minutes. Stars above, you know how to get yourself into trouble, [pc.name]. There was one, ONE zaika flying fortress and you managed to end up on board when it lifted off — I don’t know if I must be scared or impressed,” Milly says from the pilot’s seat. “By the way, we found the people you mentioned. Team C had to separate the gabilani hostage from the zaika bounty hunter. They were... extremely busy when they found them. As for the leyaks and the vulpatra bounty hunter, Team B fed emergency rations and led the former to safety before securing the latter. We came across the dryad and tauric zaika. It’ll be hard to find them a nice place to live and to keep them away from evil hands, but we’ll find a way.” “Good work.” The beta dzaan glances at [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus |Ovvi ]. “What do we do with her?” Paulette steps in. “We’ll protect her from the zaibatsu. She was Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]’s assistant, what little she remembers of her plans make her invaluable to them.” You nod in confirmation. “Gotcha,” the pilot answers. The shuttle’s door opens on Dhaal’s main spaceport. “Here you go! I hope you had a nice trip and appreciated the accommodations.” Paulette frowns. “[silly |Talk about low budget flights, no food or movies? I’m out of here! |The seats were comfortables but we could use more snacks. ]” Milly cocks an eyebrow. “You ate not one, but four bags of peanuts and your leyak drank all our soda.” “I was a little thirsty,” Dabbebieth apologizes, still wearing Paulette’s helmet. “Thanks for the ride, Milly. Take care of the E.G.G. for us.” The beta dzaan nods. “Will do.” Behind you, [flags.ZERO_PLUS_RESTORE |Zero Plus happily bounces off her seat and leaps on Paulette’s back. The former XO sighs, doing her best to stay calm with the squealing bimbo riding on her shoulders. She carries her and Dabbebieth down the ramp. You wave at the Steele Tech crew before joining the zaika. “See you around, [pc.name]. Don’t hesitate to call, if you need backup from the best recovery team of the galaxy,” Milly shouts, winking at you from inside the ship before the door closes. In the blink of an eye, the shuttle pierces the cloudy sky and soars toward the stars. You stare at it for long seconds before returning to the matter at hand: Zero Plus. “[pc.name]? Paullie Pally? Dabbidoo? Why are you making silly faces? Do I have something on my nose?” “No, it’s nothing like that. I was wondering what you wanted to do and where you wanted to go now that you are free from the E.G.G.” Zero Plus gawks at you. “I... dunno. Maybe I could come with you so we can have more fun together? Or... I could give huggies and kissies to hurt cuties? Gosh, thinking’s so hard!” |Ovvi weakly raises her head. She looks lost, tired. “What now?” Paulette places a hand on her shoulder. “[silly |Open your heart and you’ll see |Make the galaxy a better place, not just for zaika, but for everyone else ]. You’ve the body of a healer, so make use of it,” she simply says. “And if you misbehave, I’ll stalk the galaxy to correct you,” she adds. The shadow of a smile settles on Ovvi’s face. “I bet you would. Where are you taking me?” she asks Milly. “To a place where you can rebuild yourself away from the zaibatsu’s schemes.” Ovvi shrugs. “Sounds like prison.” “Don’t worry, it’s nothing like that. Our [silly |Minge |Meph-III ] outpost’s been looking for a competent nurse for months now. You won’t even need special goggles to see in the dark.” You quickly step back as the ramp raises and the turbines accelerate. Paulette stares at the rising shuttle for long seconds, following it as it pierces the clouds and reaches for the stars. When the little ship finally disappears, she finally relaxes. “So long, Dr. [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]. May you find redemption for your crimes.” You tap on her shoulder. “No need to be so dramatic.” Paulette slowly sets her eyes on you. “Correct,” she smiles, “I focused so much on her that I forgot about what truly matters,” she says, reaching for the mnemonic lock. Dabbebieth frowns. “Why did you keep it? It’s useless now.” “A little souvenir. I don’t wanna forget what happened this time. Thank you guys, for everything.” “What are you gonna do now?” you ask. “I’ll quickly visit my sisters. There’s something I need to share with them. Are you coming with lil’ guy?” Dabbebieth nods. Paulette places a peck on your right cheek. “I’ll be back soon. See you onboard, [pc.name].” With that, she deploys her wings and soars through the sky in the direction of the Little Blossoms Shelter. What an adventure! You could use a drink, or maybe two after that. There’s certainly a milk bar open nearby; there’s always one open on Dhaal. It’s good to be back in the Gyre, where only a few individuals actively want your demise. You’ll need a shower and lots of rest after all this action. Maybe Paulette will join you in bed and wind down together — she’s awfully good at snuggles for a mauve nugget. ] "])))

textify(ooe||(ooe=(0,Ce.Z)([" “What do you like to do? If you want to settle down, doing something you like is primordial.” Zero Plus takes her chin in hand, adopting her super-thinking pose. “Hum... I like huggies, healing, and mech stuff. When I look at a cutie, my shiny glasses tell me everything I wanna know about them: breast size, girth, wetness, bounciness, and all the fancy techie stuff if they’ve got chromey flashy bitsies!” Dabbebieth hovers in front of her. “Maybe you could work as a physician, or as a cybernetician? The Gyre could use a zaika doctor who doesn’t charge an arm and a leg.” The albino bimbo gasps. “Yeah, I’d like that, but I don’t feel comfi- confa- safe in here. It’s all dull and creepy.” “Let me check something,” Paulette says, tapping on her glasses. The augmented accessory flashes with light. Metadata stolen from all around the Gyre quickly slides in front of her electric blue eyes. “Bingo!” “You found something Paullie Pally?” The former XO nods. “Indeed. There’s a clinic for cyborgs nearby. It’s owned by Sabrae, a former Paragon agent. I’m sure that she’ll be glad to have a bubbly nurse by her side.” “I dunno. What if I don’t like working with her or if the place is too scawwy?” Paulette smiles. “I’ll escort you there and do the talking for you. If you don’t want to stay, I’ll find you another place to live. Worst case scenario, there’s always the Little Blossoms Shelter.” The busty doll giggles. “What’s a Lil’ Bosoms Shelter?” “A wonderful place,” Paulette replies. “Come, I’ll show you around.” Without further ado, Zero Plus takes Paulette’s hand and holds her close. The former XO extends her wings, filling the air with bright green sparkles. Dabbebieth firmly grasps her shoulder as she starts flapping her wings and activates her rocket-fitted belt. What an adventure! You could use a drink, or maybe two after that. There’s certainly a milk bar open nearby; there’s always one open on Dhaal. It’s good to be back in the Gyre, where only a few individuals actively want your demise. You’ll need a shower and lots of rest after all this action. Maybe Paulette will join you in bed and wind down together — she’s awfully good at snuggles for a mauve nugget. "])))

textify(aoe||(aoe=(0,Ce.Z)([" “There’s room on the [pc_ship.short]. We could keep her with us.” “Like, for realsies? Paullie Pally and I are gonna be roommates?” The former XO tilts her head. “I-If everyone’s fine with it, then sure.” “Will you handle it?” Dabbebieth asks. “In your shoes, I don’t know what I’d do or if I could stomach this at all.” Zero Plus gasps. “Paullie? You’ve got tummy aches? Do you need syrup?” Paulette sighs. “No, thank you. I’m fine,” she says, reaching for her hand. “Come on, let’s get you home.” “Home? I like the sound of that!” You take her other hand and guide her across the spaceport. She gasps in awe at the sight of the [pc_ship.short]. “Wooooah! It’s so big and shiny!” “And you haven’t seen how big it is on the inside,” you tease. The gushing bimbo gawks as you pass the bulkhead and guide her through the ship’s arteries. You show her the helm, the kitchen, the meeting room, and the cargo bay before bringing her to her quarters. “Is that all for me?” she asks, entering the wide room. “Yes. You can arrange and furnish it however you want.” Without a warning, the bimbo nurse jumps on the bed and bounces on it like she’s never had one before. “That’s sooooo cool! Wanna join me?” [pc.isBimbo |Heck yeah! You join her on the bed and hop together, making the mattress creak as you bounce on it without a care in the world. Stars, it’s gonna be so fun to have her onboard with you! “Paullie! Dabbidoo! Come play with us!” The former XO shakes her head. “One moment, let me put my combat stuff aside first.” Gosh, she’s been on board for five minutes and you’re already having your first sleepover! |“Maybe later,” you chuckle. “Have fun sweetie.” “Will do!” she squeals, making the mattress creak with more daring bounces. Looks like Zero Plus will be making the simple accommodations into a home pretty quickly. She’ll be a handful to manage, but you don’t doubt that she’ll be invaluable to your adventures. Paulette glances at her new roommate before reaching for her mnemonic lock device. Dabbebieth frowns. “Why did you keep it? It’s useless now.” “A little souvenir. I don’t wanna forget what happened this time. Thank you guys, for everything.” “What are you gonna do now?” you ask. “I’d like to quickly visit my sisters. There’s something I need to share with them. Are you coming with lil’ guy?” Dabbebieth nods. Paulette places a wet peck on your right cheek. “I’ll be back soon. Don’t bounce on her bed too much while I’m away. I don’t want to fix a broken mattress,” she teases. She places another kiss on your cheek before heading toward the bulkhead for a quick trip to the Little Blossoms Shelter. Stars, that was an adventure! You’ll need to rest a little after that — or to get yourself checked for any lingering injuries. Fortunately, you know a really good doctor with pretty cheap prices and a smile to die for. ] "])))

textify(noe||(noe=(0,Ce.Z)([" “We don’t need to neutralize her. We can just reset her,” you propose. Paulette cocks an eyebrow. “Are you sure?” Dabbebieth slowly floats down near the former XO. “I hate her for what she’s done to my people, to your kin, and to you, but this isn’t you! The Paulette I know would never take another’s life out of revenge[silly |ance ] — not because of a hidden flaw or whatnot, but because you are a kind soul, not a heartless machine or biomechanical monster. You’re Kemmie’s daughter, just like Lappis and Harvvey are.” Ovvi does not respond. Her body’s heavy, limp, hanging like a stringless puppet in Paulette’s hand. Reluctantly, she deactivates her energy weapon and releases the dollified doctor. Ovvi weakly flops on the floor. She gasps meekly, too concussed by her defeat to raise even a finger at you, let alone put up a fight. You lean closer and tap her cheek. “Are you still with us?” Ovvi blinks a couple of times. “You should have killed me. I’m wanted by all the zaibatsu and the things I’ve done will pursue me to the far frontier of the known universe. Whether you like it or not, you killed me G- no, Paulette. Curtains for Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ].” Paulette places her hand on Ovvi’s head. “You don’t deserve a second chance, but Zero Plus does.” The defeated Peer stays silent for a moment. She looks at you, then at her modded chest before nodding slowly. “If that’s what you want, so be it. [silly |Sayonara, Paulette the zaika |Farewell [pc.name], Dabbebieth, and you too Paulette ]. As for you, Zero Plus... I wish you the best. Carry on my legacy the way you see fit,” she murmurs before closing her eyes. The former XO doesn’t say anything. She hoists the villain over one shoulder and swifting carries her back to the main laboratory’s command throne. The mastermind is tossed on it once more, the head-plates vibrating at the exact power and frequency the central command console did. The soundwave ripples last only a couple of seconds but they feel like an eternity to you. When they stop, the entire room feels empty, devoid of life, not even your breathings can disturb its sinister coldness. D-Did it work? Your companion slowly removes the device from Ovvi’s face. She slowly kneels before her, holding her upright with her sharp hand. Dabbebieth gently ruffles her well-kept locks, trying to get a response from the unconscious doll. Her chest slowly rises and falls, but her mind’s not on — not yet, at least. She blinks a couple of times, confused. She looks at you, Paulette, and Dabbebieth with big, curious eyes. “What’s the matter friendos? Did the meanie machine hurt my brainies?” You smile. “No, everyone’s okay. You got a little sleepy so we had to do the rest without you.” Zero Plus gasps. “Wait? For realsies? Gosh, that’s so nice of you! We didn’t cross scawwy monsters on the way, did we?” “No,” Paulette instantly replies, “the trip was a little boring.” The bimbo nurse tilts her head. “Why are you covered in black dusty? Did you fly through the volcano cloudies? And why’s Dabbebieth wearing your head-thingie? Can I have it next?” Your purple crewmate bites her lips. “She tripped in the alleyway and fell into a pile of soot,” Dabbebieth replies. “More fear than harm, really.” “Oh no! Do you want me to kiss your booboos away?” Sure enough, her tits are growing with lust right away. The bimbo’s back in control, this time for good. "])))

original: textify(ioe||(ioe=(0,Ce.Z)([" The PA-X’s amorphous arms surge forward, pinning you to the wall. You try to writhe out of its shifting grasp but the fight took its toll on you. “[pc.name]! Noooooooooooo!” Dabbebieth wails. A second later, the [silly |Ultimate Lifeform |PA-X ] captures him in an electromagnetic bubble. The cobalt alien shakes in his crackling prison, doing everything he can to escape. Ovvi chuckles from her desk. “Impressive! I expected you to go down faster,” the albino snarls, reaching for the captured leyak. “My Subjugator could use a determined pilot like you. textify(soe||(soe=(0,Ce.Z)(["","An alert chirps on your Codex. The screen turns on the U.G.C’s main news channel. A concerned cylirian host forcibly smiles at the camera, trying her best to stay calm in spite of the situation. “Breaking News! A giant biomorph has been spotted above the Rush-world known as Dhaal!” The image switches to a blurry picture of a titanic beast. It’s larger than a capital ship, with a hard, metallic skeleton visible through its translucent, jelly-like skin. A trio of bright red eyes stare at you through the screen, towering over a maw filled with gleaming teeth. Its large, crackling tentacles crush tiny satellites, grinding them down into dust that its powerful sails scatter all around its angular sides. “It wrecked three escort ships before breaking a battlecruiser in two with a single breath of bioplasma. The creature then disappeared in thin air, leaving bristled wrecks in its wake. Be extremely cautious - there’s no way to know when and where this leviathan will strike next!” The screen flickers a couple of times before looping the message. You pause the video the moment the creature appears on screen. The picture is grainy, as if the light was bent by electromagnetic forces. Its skeleton looks awfully similar to the E.G.G in size and shape, albeit with a more organic side. No, it can’t be! This is ridiculous! It must be a coincidence - the galaxy is immense, inhabited with thousands of unknown species... This must be a completely new form of life, not some new form of bio-engineered behemoth birthed from Ovvi’s sick mind! You’ll have to be careful. If this thing is as dangerous as the news pretends it is, you’ll have to travel with extreme caution."])),blockHeader("Alert!"))

textify(roe||(roe=(0,Ce.Z)(["","The stars twinkle in front of you. Suddenly, a dark vortex appears, swallowing the light in its dark horizon. The QCOMM systems activate, blaring an otherworldly wail in the helm. A moment later, a huge, gelatinous head surges from the cosmic anomaly. Teeth the size of skyscrapers devour stellar dust, chugging photons and debris in a cavernous maw. Crackling tentacles and miles-long sails emerge from the primordial soup just before a trio of bright red eyes. Glossy eyelids slowly open, revealing fiery orbs and pupils as dark as the void itself. The Space Leviathan roars, belching plasma through its blowhole, filling the cockpit with kaleidoscopic lights. Electromagnetic clouds and stellar lightning crackle around its wobbling length, wreathing its iridescent sails and translucent tentacles in shimmering auroras as it sets its wrathful gaze on you! It’s a fight!"])),blockHeader("Space Leviathan!"))

textify(uoe||(uoe=(0,Ce.Z)(["","The Space Leviathan careens. It writhes weakly, too hurt and stunned to fight back! Its translucent skin bears many cuts and bruises and you can clearly see some fractures in its metallic skeleton.

		You fire your weapons again, dazing the beast with a direct hit to the head!

		The gelatinous behemoth’s lights fade. It slowly drifts in the void, spinning like a damaged satellite about to escape its orbit. You won’t kill the bio-mechanical titan with your current loadout, but this should knock it down for some time.

		What now? You can leave it here and risk meeting it again, or call the Peacekeepers in the hopes that they’ll contain the beast - though putting them in danger to tame it could turn deadly. The choice is up to you!"])),blockHeader("Victory!"))

textify(loe||(loe=(0,Ce.Z)(["","You reach for the QCOMMs and dial the nearest Peacekeeper outpost. “U.G.C cruisers, do you hear me?” The loudspeakers crackle. “Good day citizen. What’s your emergency?” You activate your ship’s sensors and link the live feed to the communication system. “I came across the Space Leviathan from the news. It’s stunned and weakened right now, but it’s only a matter of time until it disappears back into the void. If you want to tame it, it’s now or never!” A moment passes before the buzzing voice answers. “An interception fleet is heading to your coordinates. Leave the sector immediately - we’ll handle the rest ourselves. We owe you a big one, Captain Steele.” You take a picture of the stunned beast as a souvenir before steering out of range. Pfffew! That was a close one! You don’t know if it’s bad luck or if it’s got something against you in particular, but the leviathan was particularly furious and stubborn in your presence. Maybe it’s karma biting you back in the ass for your past cowardice? In the end, it doesn’t matter. You survived insurmountable odds once again!"])),blockHeader("Peacekeepers Arrive"))
